official_name: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
notes: OECD provides international AI governance frameworks including AI risk classification, incident monitoring, and a common incident reporting framework.

data.official_name: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
data.description: Framework for classifying AI systems by dimensions such as autonomy level, data input type, and application domain.
data.notes: Provides a structured classification of AI systems along multiple dimensions. Used by OECD member countries for policy development and risk assessment. No per-item identifiers — dimensions and categories are described in the framework document.

data.description: Database monitoring AI incidents and hazards reported across OECD member countries.
data.notes: Tracks AI incidents and near-misses. Provides categorization of incident types. No formal per-item identifiers for categories — the database uses its own internal structure.

data.official_name: OECD Common Framework for AI Incident Reporting
data.description: Common framework for reporting AI risks and incidents, defining standardized terminology for hazards and harms.
data.notes: Defines common terminology and structure for AI incident reporting across jurisdictions. Part of OECD's broader AI risk and incidents work. No per-item identifiers — concepts are described in the framework document.
